summary: Closed 5 AST coverage gaps caught by W1.5-M6 safety net during the rolled-back W2-ATOMIC dry run. Investigation revealed the gaps were NOT missing AST source/sink entries (all 4 ostensibly-missing entries already existed in their respective banks). The gaps were 2 deeper architectural couplings between the AST detection path and the regex banks. Fixes are additive (regex banks remain active); Wave-2-atomic can now safely delete the regex banks.

fix_1_call_shape_member_pattern_match.description: Extended member_patterns_match() to also match call-shaped descendants (Java method_invocation, TS/JS/Go/Rust/C# call_expression, etc.). When extract_call_name returns a dotted form like 'request.getParameter', the function now splits on the LAST '.' and matches it against (receiver, field) member_patterns. Previously these only matched against extract_member_access_receiver_and_field which returns None for call nodes (calls are NOT field_access nodes in tree-sitter Java/TS/Go grammars). The structural+wildcard semantics from the existing path are preserved (receiver=='*' wildcard, exact-receiver match).

fix_2_regex_free_var_extraction.description: Added two regex-free var-extraction helpers that traverse the AST node directly. extract_first_identifier_arg_ast walks the descendant's `arguments` field (or positional fallback) and returns the first identifier-shaped child, skipping string literals. extract_assignment_rhs_ident does a pure-text scan for the LAST `=` (skipping `==`/`!=`/`<=`/`>=`) and walks forward, with object-literal-key skip for JSX `={{ __html: tainted }}` shape. Both helpers are wired as final-position .or_else() fallbacks in detect_sources_ast (sources) and detect_sinks_ast (sinks) AFTER the existing regex-bank var extraction, so additive mode behaves identically.

implemented.ast_bank_changes: NONE. All 4 putatively-missing AST entries (C fgets, Java request.getParameter, Kotlin Runtime.exec, Swift Process.run) were already present at HEAD c874e7d. The orchestrator's Step 2 was based on a misreading of the recovery capture — the failures were architectural, not missing-entries.

discoveries.primary_finding: Load-bearing regex coupling in the AST var-extraction path
discoveries.details: Both detect_sources_ast and detect_sinks_ast in compute_taint_with_tree call get_patterns(language) (the regex bank) to derive a regex pattern, then pass it to extract_call_arg / extract_sink_var_from_statement to extract the tainted variable. extract_call_arg uses pattern.find(statement) to locate the start of the call's arguments. When Wave-2-atomic deletes the regex bank, get_patterns returns empty .sources/.sinks vectors, regex_patterns.sinks.iter().find(...) returns None, var = None, and the AST hit is silently dropped at `if let Some(var)` — even though the AST detection itself succeeded. This was the root cause of all 5 W2-ATOMIC regressions. The 4 'missing AST entries' from the orchestrator brief were a red herring; the entries existed but the var-extraction step couldn't supply a variable.
discoveries.details_2: extract_member_access_receiver_and_field dispatches via field_access_info(language) which only knows about field_access / member_expression / selector_expression / navigation_expression node kinds. In Java tree-sitter, `request.getParameter(...)` is a single method_invocation node — not a field_access. So `('request', 'getParameter')` member_patterns matched zero descendants. The regex bank caught the line via substring; the AST member_pattern was effectively dead. Fix: derive (receiver, field) from extract_call_name's dotted form for call-shaped nodes, then match against the same (receiver, field) member_patterns table.
discoveries.wave_2_atomic_implication: The 2 fixes here close the architectural gaps. Wave-2-atomic can now delete the regex banks atomically. The remaining regex-bank consumers in detect_sinks_ast (extract_call_arg + extract_sink_var_from_statement) are KEPT AS-IS to preserve additive-mode behavior — they harmlessly become no-ops once the bank is empty, and the new AST-only fallbacks (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast, extract_assignment_rhs_ident) take over. No changes needed in compute_taint_with_tree's dispatch loop for Wave-2-atomic.

tests.additive_mode_summary: rr_baseline_per_language_test: 16/16 PASS. rr_framework_integ_test: 18/18 PASS. All 34 W1.5 integration tests green.
tests.ast_only_mode_simulation_summary: Temporarily disabled regex fallback in compute_taint_with_tree (then restored before commit). Re-ran all 34 tests: 34/34 PASS under AST-only dispatch. Confirms Wave-2-atomic safety: deleting the regex banks will not regress these 34 tests.

full_test_suite.result: PASS for the targeted 5 + all 34 W1.5 integration tests; pre-existing orthogonal failures unchanged.
pre_existing_orthogonal_failures: (4 doctest failures in callgraph/cross_file_types, callgraph::languages::kotlin/luau, surface::triggers)
full_test_suite.verification: Diffed failing-test names against W2-ATOMIC-recovery-capture.txt. All 15 unique W2-pre failures are a STRICT SUBSET of W2-ATOMIC's 20 failures. The 5 missing from W2-pre are exactly the 5 baseline tests this milestone fixed (c_baseline, java_baseline, kotlin_baseline, swift_baseline, nextjs_dangerously). Zero new regressions introduced.

issues.pre_existing_unchanged: 16 unique pre-existing failures (file system, git, language parity, quality metrics, doctests, change-impact-git, AST-tree-empty-dir). All present at HEAD c874e7d. Orthogonal to taint analysis. Will be addressed in a separate hygiene milestone.
issues.blockers_encountered: None. The 'gaps' described in the orchestrator brief (missing AST entries) were a misreading; once the actual root cause (regex coupling) was identified, fixes were straightforward.

wave_2_atomic_readiness: READY. The architectural couplings that caused the W2-ATOMIC dry-run regressions are now closed. Wave-2-atomic deletion of regex banks should leave all 34 W1.5 integration tests green.
